title: Mycelium RNA Extraction and Library Prep
author: Chidrupi Golla
materials: Thaw the following reagents and spin down briefly using a microfuge, before mixing as indicated in the table below, and place on ice. %s %s RNaseOUT™ Recombinant Ribonuclease Inhibitor Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10777019 Maxima H Minus Reverse Transcriptase Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog ##EP0741
Thaw the following reagents and spin down briefly using a microfuge, before mixing as indicated in the table below, and place on ice. %s %s
Combine the following reagents in a 0.2 ml PCR tube: %s %s
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the RNA pellets
In a separate tube, mix the following together: %s %s RNaseOUT™ Recombinant Ribonuclease Inhibitor Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10777019

steps.title: Sieve collected soil samples through a...
steps.instruction: Sieve collected soil samples through a 2-mm sieve to remove any root fragments

steps.title: Disperse a 5 g soil sample...
steps.instruction: Disperse a 5 g soil sample into 100 mL of deionized water

steps.title: Stir the mixture at 500 rpm...
steps.instruction: Stir the mixture at 500 rpm using a magnetic stirrer for 00:05:00

steps.title: Filter the mixture through a 1000 µm pore nylon mesh
steps.instruction: Filter the mixture through a 1000 µm pore nylon mesh

steps.title: Thorough wash the oversized particles with...
steps.instruction: Thorough wash the oversized particles with 100 mL of deionized water

steps.title: Repeat steps 4-5 with the filtrate
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4-5 with the filtrate

steps.title: Filter the filtrate twice through a...
steps.instruction: Filter the filtrate twice through a 50 µm pore metal sleeve containing a 50 µm pore nylon mesh

steps.title: Discard the filtrate and collect the...
steps.instruction: Discard the filtrate and collect the oversized particles in a 50 mL tube by rinsing them using 35 mL of deionized water for storage if needed (store at 4℃)

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples for 00:03:00 at 31000xg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples for 00:03:00 at 31000xg

steps.title: Disperse the pellet by adding 50 mL of 45
steps.instruction: Disperse the pellet by adding 50 mL of 45.5% sucrose solution and hand-shaking vigorously

steps.title: Centrifuge the solution at 50 x g, °C, for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the solution at 50 x g, °C, for 00:01:00

steps.title: Filter the supernatant through a three-layered...
steps.instruction: Filter the supernatant through a three-layered 50 µm pore size nylon mesh

steps.title: Repeat steps 9-13 four more times
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 9-13 four more times

steps.title: Thoroughly wash the particles collected on...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly wash the particles collected on the nylon mesh with deionized water. Make sure to rinse out the sucrose solution

steps.title: Collect the particles into a 3...
steps.instruction: Collect the particles into a 3 mL Eppendorf tube and allow them to dry completely

steps.title: Store between 20-30 °C if needed
steps.instruction: Store between 20-30 °C if needed.

steps.title: Ground the particles into a fine powder in liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: Ground the particles into a fine powder in liquid nitrogen

steps.title: Add 2 mL of TRIzol Reagent...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of TRIzol Reagent Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15596026 and ground the sample further until the slurry is thawed

steps.title: Split the sample into two 2 mL Eppendorf tubes
steps.instruction: Split the sample into two 2 mL Eppendorf tubes

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Add 300 µL of chloroform and...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL of chloroform and sahe the tube vigorously for 15 seconds

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:03:00

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 13
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 13.000 x g, °C, for 00:15:00 at 4 °C

steps.title: Repeat steps 5-7
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 5-7

steps.title: Precipitate the RNA using 500 µL...
steps.instruction: Precipitate the RNA using 500 µL of isopropanol at -20 °C for 02:00:00

steps.title: Collect the precipitated RNA through centrifugation...
steps.instruction: Collect the precipitated RNA through centrifugation at 13000 x g, °C, for 00:15:00 at 4 °C

steps.title: Quantify the RNA using the Qubit and Nanodrop fluorometers
steps.instruction: Quantify the RNA using the Qubit and Nanodrop fluorometers

steps.title: Prepare the RNA in nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Prepare the RNA in nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Add 1 µg of total RNA to a 0
steps.instruction: Add 1 µg of total RNA to a 0.2 ml PCR tube

steps.title: Adjust the volume to 7
steps.instruction: Adjust the volume to 7.5 µL with nuclease-free water

steps.title: Mix the tube by flicking
steps.instruction: Mix the tube by flicking

steps.title: Spin down in a microfuge
steps.instruction: Spin down in a microfuge

steps.title: Prepare the following reaction in the 0
steps.instruction: Prepare the following reaction in the 0.2 ml PCR tube containing the prepared RNA input: %s %s

steps.title: Mix gently by flicking the tube and then spin down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by flicking the tube and then spin down

steps.title: Incubate at 60 °C for 00:05:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 60 °C for 00:05:00 and then snap cool on a pre-chilled freezer block for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down.

steps.title: Add the 8 µL of strand-switching...
steps.instruction: Add the 8 µL of strand-switching reagents (prepared in steps 6-7) to the 11 µL of snap-cooled mRNA (from steps 2-5). Mix by flicking the tube and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate at 42 °C for 00:02:00 in the thermal cycler
steps.instruction: Incubate at 42 °C for 00:02:00 in the thermal cycler.

steps.title: Add 1 µl of Maxima H...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl of Maxima H Minus Reverse Transcriptase Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog ##EP0741 . The total volume is now 20 µL .

steps.title: Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate using the following protocol using a thermal cycler
steps.instruction: Incubate using the following protocol using a thermal cycler: %s %s

steps.title: Thaw the AMPure XP Beads (AXP)...
steps.instruction: Thaw the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) at room temperature and mix by vortexing. Keep the beads at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 1 µL RNase Cocktail Enzyme...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL RNase Cocktail Enzyme Mix (ThermoFisher, cat # AM2286) to the reverse transcription reaction.

steps.title: Incubate the reaction for 00:10:00 at...
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction for 00:10:00 at 37 °C in a thermal cycler.

steps.title: Resuspend the AMPure XP beads (AXP) by vortexing
steps.instruction: Resuspend the AMPure XP beads (AXP) by vortexing.

steps.title: Transfer the sample to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: Add 17 µL of resuspended AMPure...
steps.instruction: Add 17 µL of resuspended AMPure XP beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix by flicking the tube.

steps.title: 0 µL Incubate on a Hula...
steps.instruction: 0 µL Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Prepare 500 µL of fresh 80% ethanol in nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Prepare 500 µL of fresh 80% ethanol in nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet
steps.instruction: Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet. Keep the tube on the magnet, and pipette off the supernatant.

steps.title: Keep the tubes on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Keep the tubes on the magnet and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol without disturbing the pellet. Remove the ethanol using a pipette and discard. Note: If the pellet was disturbed, wait for beads to pellet again before removing the ethanol.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step.

steps.title: Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet
steps.instruction: Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet. Pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow to dry for ~ 00:00:30 , but do not dry the pellet to the point of cracking.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend pellet in 20 µL nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 00:10:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Briefly spin down the tube and...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the tube and pellet the beads on the magnet until the eluate is clear and colorless, for at least 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Remove and retain 20 µL of...
steps.instruction: Remove and retain 20 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.

steps.instruction: Prepare the following reaction in a 0.2 ml thin-walled PCR tube: %s %s

steps.instruction: Quantify the DNA using a Agilent Bioanalyzer and Qubit Fluorometer

steps.title: Prepare the NEBNext Ultra II End...
steps.instruction: Prepare the NEBNext Ultra II End Repair / dA-tailing Module reagents in accordance with manufacturer's instructions, and place on ice.

steps.title: Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently pipetting and briefly spinning down.

steps.title: Using a thermal cycler, incubate at...
steps.instruction: Using a thermal cycler, incubate at 20 °C for 00:05:00 and 65 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.instruction: Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing.

steps.title: Remove and retain 61 µL of...
steps.instruction: Remove and retain 61 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: Spin down the Ligation Adapter (LA)...
steps.instruction: Spin down the Ligation Adapter (LA) and Quick T4 Ligase, and place on ice.

steps.title: Thaw Ligation Buffer (LNB) at room...
steps.instruction: Thaw Ligation Buffer (LNB) at room temperature, spin down and mix by pipetting. Due to viscosity, vortexing this buffer is ineffective. Place on ice immediately after thawing and mixing.

steps.title: Thaw the Elution Buffer (EB) at...
steps.instruction: Thaw the Elution Buffer (EB) at room temperature and mix by vortexing. Then spin down and place on ice.

steps.title: Thaw the Short Fragment Buffer (SFB)...
steps.instruction: Thaw the Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) at room temperature and mix by vortexing. Then spin down and place on ice.

steps.title: In a 1
steps.instruction: In a 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, mix in the following order: Between each addition, pipette mix 10-20 times %s %s

steps.title: Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently pipetting and briefly spinning down.

steps.title: Incubate the reaction for 00:10:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction for 00:10:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing
steps.instruction: Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing.

steps.title: Add 40 µl of resuspended AMPure...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µl of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix by flicking the tube.

steps.title: Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet
steps.instruction: Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet. Keep the tube on the magnet, and pipette off the supernatant when clear and colorless.

steps.title: Wash the beads by adding 250...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads by adding 250 µL of Short Fragment Buffer (SFB). Flick the beads to resuspend, spin down, then return the tube to the magnetic rack and allow the beads to pellet. Remove the supernatant using a pipette and discard. Take care when removing the supernatant, the viscosity of the buffer can contribute to loss of beads from the pellet.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step.

steps.title: Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet
steps.instruction: Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet. Pipette off any residual supernatant. Allow to dry for ~ 00:00:30 , but do not dry the pellet to the point of cracking.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 15 µL Elution Buffer (EB). Spin down and incubate for 00:10:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Pellet the beads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colorless, for at least 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Remove and retain 15 µL of...
steps.instruction: Remove and retain 15 µL of eluate containing the DNA library into a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: Quantify 1 µL of the eluted...
steps.instruction: Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using a Qubit fluorometer

steps.title: Depending on your DNA library fragment...
steps.instruction: Depending on your DNA library fragment size, prepare your final library in 12 µL of Elution Buffer (EB). %s %s

steps.title: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library...
steps.instruction: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library Beads (LIB) or Library Solution (LIS, if using), Flow Cell Tether (FCT) and Flow Cell Flush (FCF) at room temperature before mixing by vortexing. Then spin down and store on ice.

steps.title: To prepare the flow cell priming...
steps.instruction: To prepare the flow cell priming mix with BSA, combine Flow Cell Flush (FCF) and Flow Cell Tether (FCT), as directed below. Mix by pipetting at room temperature. %s %s BSA is not required but improves sequencing

steps.title: Open the MinION or GridION device...
steps.instruction: Open the MinION or GridION device lid and slide the flow cell under the clip. Press down firmly on the flow cell to ensure correct thermal and electrical contact.

steps.title: Slide the flow cell priming port...
steps.instruction: Slide the flow cell priming port cover clockwise to open the priming port.

steps.title: After opening the priming port, check...
steps.instruction: After opening the priming port, check for a small air bubble under the cover. Draw back a small volume to remove any bubbles:

steps.title: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µL
steps.instruction: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µL

steps.title: Insert the tip into the priming port
steps.instruction: Insert the tip into the priming port

steps.title: Turn the wheel until the dial...
steps.instruction: Turn the wheel until the dial shows 220-230 µl, to draw back 20-30 µl, or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip

steps.title: Load 800 µL of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 800 µL of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port, avoiding the introduction of air bubbles. Wait for five minutes. During this time, prepare the library for loading by following the steps below.

steps.title: Thoroughly mix the contents of the...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix the contents of the Library Beads (LIB) by pipetting.

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, prepare the library for loading as follows: %s %s

steps.title: Gently lift the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently lift the SpotON sample port cover to make the SpotON sample port accessible.

steps.title: Load 200 µL of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 200 µL of the priming mix into the flow cell priming port (not the SpotON sample port), avoiding the introduction of air bubbles.

steps.title: Mix the prepared library gently by...
steps.instruction: Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting up and down just prior to loading.

steps.title: Add 75 μl of the prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 75 μl of the prepared library to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion. Ensure each drop flows into the port before adding the next.

steps.title: Gently replace the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover, making sure the bung enters the SpotON port and close the priming port.

steps.title: Place the light shield onto the flow cell, as follows:
steps.instruction: Place the light shield onto the flow cell, as follows:

steps.title: Carefully place the leading edge of...
steps.instruction: Carefully place the leading edge of the light shield against the clip. Note: Do not force the light shield underneath the clip.

steps.title: Gently lower the light shield onto the flow cell
steps.instruction: Gently lower the light shield onto the flow cell. The light shield should sit around the SpotON cover, covering the entire top section of the flow cell.

steps.title: Close the device lid and set...
steps.instruction: Close the device lid and set up a sequencing run on MinKNOW
